name: Transformers: The Game
post_apply_message:
Transformers: The Game (2007, non-Steam, abandonware) — setup:
1. Game folder on the SD card (GFM detects it).
2. Apply this fix — it AUTO-ADDS the Steam shortcut (Transformers.exe, start dir = game folder, gospel appid). Steam closes briefly to write it, and it sets GE-Proton 10.34 (install it via ProtonUp-Qt first — Steam won't auto-fetch GE builds; if the mapping shows missing, that's why).
3. Tony's copy is a DRM-cracked/abandonware build (SafeDisc removed), so it launches under Proton with no Uplay/disc check — just run it.
4. If you hit missing-DLL errors, the crack ships redistributables in _Redist.
CONTROLLER: the game supports an XInput gamepad natively. If it only registers keyboard, that's Steam Input mapping your pad to keys — in Steam open the Transformers controller settings and switch the layout to 'Gamepad' (pass the Xbox controller straight through) instead of a keyboard/mouse template. This is a Steam Input setting, not something GFM can write.
RESOLUTION: this is a UE3 game; a widescreen/1920x1200 tweak lives in its config ini, but I haven't confirmed the exact file/keys for the 2007 title yet — tell me if you want it and I'll dig into the on-disk config once it's mapped.
notes: Transformers: The Game (Traveller's Tales, 2007, UE3). Delisted / never digital; Tony's copy is a cracked abandonware build (ChemicalFlood repack; NAS \192.168.1.33\Rom_Library\roms\win\Transformers The Game.zip). Launch exe per the crack's _HOW TO RUN GAME!!.txt = Transformers.exe ("run as administrator"). SafeDisc DRM already removed so it runs under Proton. Documentation-only recipe for now (registers the game for detection/mapping + captures the run method); a UE3 resolution ini step (1920x1200) can be added once the exact config path is confirmed from the installed copy. Related but separate game also on the NAS: Transformers 2 / Revenge of the Fallen (Transformers2.exe) + Transformers: War for Cybertron (existing twfc recipe).
